title: Tripleurospermum rupestre
intro: Tripleurospermum rupestre, also known as Alpine Mayweed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tripleurospermum rupestre include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alpine Mayweed is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Tripleurospermum rupestre is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is part of the genus Tripleurospermum, which includes various species of plants commonly known as salsify or hawk's beard. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Tripleurospermum rupestre is closely related to other plants like daisies and sunflowers. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
